$comment: SHOP MY LOOK — the whole business case, in one feature.
After a wedding, Caitlin ticks the products she actually used on that bride, names it,
and publishes. She gets a link like /looks/sarah-june-2026.html and texts it to her.
Every chair she fills becomes recurring affiliate income instead of a one-off fee.
WHY IT'S A PAGE ON HER SITE AND NOT A LIST OF LINKS IN A TEXT MESSAGE:
Amazon's Operating Agreement restricts affiliate links in SMS/DM to *solicited* messages.
Texting a bride a raw Amazon link is legally grey. Texting her a link to a public page on
Caitlin's own domain is completely safe. So the page IS the compliance strategy.
TIMING MATTERS: Amazon's cookie is only 24 HOURS. Send it the same day, while she's still
buzzing about her face. A week later it earns nothing.
Look pages are PUBLIC but noindex — unlisted, not password-protected. Password-protecting
them would fail Amazon's 'publicly available' requirement.
productIds must match ids in products.json. A product that isn't 'live' is skipped.
